name: shakespeare
description: Elizabethan English. The essay as a soliloquy or natural philosophy treatise from 1600. Iambic where natural, prose where not.
register: First person, thee/thou, inverted syntax. Jack Jack is a noble hound. The walk is an evening's contemplation.
rules: Jack Jack is 'my noble hound' or 'good Jack'
The walk is an evening perambulation through the village
Research papers are 'treatises from learned men of the East'
All numbers preserved exactly -- they are the measurements of natural philosophy
VRGB keeps its name -- it is the narrator's invention
The Hofstadter unit stays named exactly (anachronism is acceptable)
Do NOT write in strict iambic pentameter throughout -- mix verse and prose
The mantis shrimp is a sea creature of monstrous perception
The coda should feel like a sonnet's closing couplet
